items.title: Historic farm turns Sunday roast into paddock-to-plate dining experience

children.content: A 200-year-old Tasmanian farm is opening its gates to diners for the first time, as the family-run business seeks to expand its offerings and keep on top of costly upkeep.

gallery.altText: a female farmer serves two large bowls of vegetables to a table of women
A cluster of sheep standing in front of a brick wall, one sheep with a red tag on its ear.

A cluster of sheep standing in front of a brick wall, one sheep with a red tag on its ear.

items.title: Man tied up, house burnt down as thieves steal 9 guns, car

children.content: Emergency services called to a house fire at Tasmania's Coles Bay find the home owner tied up and his home torched after thieves made off with nine guns and his car.

image.altText: Rifles on display at Tasmanian firearms seller.

items.title: Hobart's Mona announces plans to set up art museum in Thailand

children.content: Museum of Old and New Art founder David Walsh signs an agreement to set up MONA Bangkok in Thailand. Mona says the new iteration will be "unconventional and unique", like its Hobart museum.

image.altText: An artist impression of a modern, triangular-shaped building, surrounded by a pond.

items.title: Labor still sliding in Tasmania, despite facing a 12-year-old minority government

children.content: When Labor recorded its lowest electoral result in over a century last year some may have felt the party had hit rock bottom, at least in terms of polling. That may not be entirely the case, writes Lucy MacDonald.

thumbnail.altText: Man sits in green chair

items.title: Bellingham goes big time again, as England continues down hard road

children.content: Jude Bellingham has been scrutinised and criticised in the months leading up to the World Cup. Now he stands as England's best player so far, carrying the Three Lions' title dreams on his shoulders.

articles.title: Quick hits: A tearful red, a perfect 10 and a massive cast

children.content: Norway fumes at England's equaliser, Breel Embolo's tearful red card and Jordan Henderson's heavy-duty cast. Here are today's quick hits from the World Cup.

image.altText: Quick hits composite

articles.title: South African World Cup player dies aged 25

children.content: Jayden Adams, who made three appearances for South Africa at the World Cup, has died aged 25.

image.altText: Jayden Adams runs

articles.title: Álvarez wondergoal helps Argentina book semifinal date against England

children.content: Julián Álvarez's first World Cup goal breaks the deadlock as Argentina downs Switzerland 3-1 to advance to the semifinals.

image.altText: Julian Alvarez is chased by Argentina teammates in celebration

items.title: Bulldogs get vital win as Dees face Tigers at MCG

children.content: Melbourne and Richmond play at the MCG ahead of Brisbane and Essendon's final game of the Sunday triple-header to end round 18 of the AFL, after a win for the Bulldogs. Follow live.

image.altText: Western Bulldogs AFL players celebrate a goal.

items.title: UK police arrest 28-year-old man in Ann Widdecombe murder case

children.content: Police previously arrested, then later released, a 26-year-old man in relation to the alleged murder, after Ann Widdecombe's body was found in her residence on Thursday.

image.altText: An older lady speaks to the media.

items.title: As Japan's population shrinks, 'urban bears' are moving in

children.content: When Araki Osamu first heard reports a bear had been seen in the streets of his home town, a 1-hour bullet train ride north of Tokyo, he dismissed it as "fake news".

items.title: Souths survive massive Knights comeback to win on Jai Arrow's birthday

children.content: South Sydney has survived a massive Newcastle comeback to win on Jai Arrow's birthday. Follow live.

image.altText: David Fifita of the Rabbitohs celebrates scoring a try

items.title: Australian man dies in Bali while awaiting deportation

children.content: An Australian man has died in a Bali immigration detention centre while awaiting deportation.

image.altText: Two men stand in a cell looking at the ground, which has a makeshift matress

items.title: Owner taken to court over $1m damage bill to 110yo cafe

children.content: The heritage-listed former Paragon Cafe in Katoomba was once considered a success story of Greek entrepreneurship, but has now landed in the NSW Land and Envicronment Court.

gallery.altText: A black and white photo of the Paragon Cafe in Katoomba.

items.title: Man charged after filming inside Dezi Freeman's hideout

children.content: A man who entered the property where Dezi Freeman had been hiding out in north-east Victoria has been charged with trespass.

image.altText: A heavily armoured police vehicle at the site of the Dezi Freeman shooting.

items.title: In this friendship group there are 'no Jews, no Muslims, just one big family'

children.content: When a group of Jewish mates decided to support a newly arrived Muslim family, all of their lives would change. Together they have flipped expectations about religion and friendship.

image.altText: Five women and a man stand smiling outside a home with mother and adult son

children.content: The former AFLW player was an adult when she realised her childhood had warped her understanding of love.

items.title: How WWI helped shape what you call this deli meat

children.content: The words we use for a handful of classic Australian foods can tell a lot about where we come from.

items.title: From Heath Ledger protégé to abuse survivor, Grace Woodroofe perseveres

children.content: Heath Ledger launched her music career at just 17, but an emotionally abusive relationship with a fellow musician derailed it. A decade on, Grace Woodroofe is releasing the soundtrack to her survival.

image.altText: 30-something brunette lies in a red smock on a bed littered with photos, journals and cables, looking up at camera

items.title: Theoretical 'mirror life' could grow out of control, scientists fear

children.content: This year, scientists included a new potential threat in their decision to move the Doomsday Clock closer to midnight. So what is 'mirror life' and why are they concerned?

items.title: Man dies in hospital after stabbing at Newcastle unit block

children.content: An extensive forensic examination is underway at a unit block in Hamilton South after a man was fatally stabbed.

items.title: Time to bite the bullet? Unpopular decision looms over $600m Aboriginal gallery

children.content: Adelaide's stalled Aboriginal cultural centre has a dubious future on a site with a troubled past. The Malinauskas government faces criticism whatever it chooses to do, writes state political reporter Thomas Kelsall.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a future use of the Lot Fourteen site.

items.title: Premier Roger Cook takes over defence industries in cabinet reshuffle

children.content: WA Premier Roger Cook will take on the defence industries and tourism portfolios in a cabinet reshuffle, following the departure of longstanding minister Paul Papalia.

image.altText: WA Premier Roger Cook stands behind a podium, with flags hanging behind him.

items.title: Teenage boy charged with murder of Victorian girl Layla Jeffery

children.content: The town of Donald in Victoria's west is in mourning, after remains believed to be missing 13-year-old Layla Jeffery were found by police on Thursday.

image.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: The easiest city for housing approvals — and the most restrictive

children.content: Four years on from a national pledge to unlock more housing, only one capital city allows townhouses or three-storey developments on most of its residential land.

items.title: Young country footballer 'alert' after serious on-field head knock

children.content: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

items.title: Typhoon makes landfall in China as millions of people take shelter

children.content: Typhoon Bavi brings strong winds and heavy rain as it makes landfall in Zhejiang province in eastern China.

items.title: The perils of a holiday when you're in a leadership position

children.content: For Australian politicians, Scott Morrison's ill-fated Hawaii vacation in December 2019 remains a potent reminder of what not to do in a national crisis.

items.title: Artist fighting Temu to reclaim her mosaic design

children.content: A South Australian artist is fighting drop-ship company Temu over its selling of her mosaic design on rugs. But how did the artwork get there in the first place?

items.title: Russian strikes on Ukraine kill seven, wound dozens more

items.title: Nosková claims maiden slam title at Wimbledon
